compiled_state.title: Ticket-only sessions lose changes — tickets branch not pushed to origin when no code changes are made
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description: When a Claude Code web session only modifies tickets (e.g., brainstorming new epics via /dso:brainstorm, creating bugs, enriching ticket descriptions) without making any code changes, the local tickets branch is never pushed to origin. Web sessions run in temporary environments that are destroyed after the session ends, so all ticket changes (new epics, comments, priority updates, description enrichments) are permanently lost. This is especially impactful for brainstorm sessions, which are frequently run in web environments specifically to ideate and create well-defined epics. The /dso:end skill and commit workflow focus on code changes — there is no mechanism to detect and push ticket-only changes before session teardown. Expected behavior: sessions that modify the tickets branch should push those changes to origin before session close, even when no code changes were made.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 3 (INTERMEDIATE). severity=1, complexity=1, environment=1, intermittent=0.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). Proceeding to investigation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: SAFEGUARD APPROVED: user approved editing end-session/SKILL.md. Fix: add explicit tickets branch push independent of code push path.
